title: Uninitialized return value in validate function
reason: Incorrect. In Solidity 0.8, the named return variable `valid` is default-initialized to `false`; the guarded branch does not return random or uninitialized data.

title: Missing access control on pokeFailedOverPrice
reason: Permissionless access is intentional and only becomes dangerous because failover switches the oracle to the TWAP source. That risk is already captured in F-002; the timing-only aspect is not a separate reportable issue.

title: No reverts on invalid or non-existent Uniswap pools
reason: The claim is misstated: bad pool addresses will generally revert once `observe()` is called. The real issue is missing constructor validation of the intended pool identity, which is captured more accurately in F-005.

title: Potential precision loss in TWAP calculation
reason: Minor integer rounding from tick averaging is expected in fixed-point oracle math and does not create a realistic exploit or protocol-level harm by itself.

title: Off-by-one in anchor ratio bounds
reason: Incorrect. When tolerance is zero, both bounds equal `1e18`, and an exact reporter/anchor match passes because the check is `<= upper` and `>= lower`.
